evals.name: Minimal Harness Creation — TypeScript + React
evals.prompt: I have a TypeScript + React project at /tmp/eval-target with no agent setup. Scaffold a minimal harness so an agent can work on it reliably.
evals.expected_output: Invokes create-harness.mjs with --target /tmp/eval-target, detects Node stack via package.json, produces AGENTS.md, feature_list.json with 5 placeholder features, progress.md, session-handoff.md, and init.sh with bun/npm install + typecheck + test + build commands. Reports detected stack and which files were written.
expectations: Runs `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/create-harness.mjs --target /tmp/eval-target` (with or without additional flags)
Reports the detected stack (Node / TypeScript / React) back to the user
feature_list.json contains 5 placeholder feature entries with id, name, description, status fields
init.sh contains npm/bun/pnpm verification commands appropriate for the package manager, not Go or Python commands
Lists which of AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, session-handoff.md, init.sh were written vs skipped

evals.name: Audit Existing AGENTS.md
evals.prompt: I have an existing AGENTS.md at /tmp/eval-existing-harness but my agent still breaks things. Audit the harness and tell me what to improve first.
evals.expected_output: Invokes validate-harness.mjs with --target /tmp/eval-existing-harness --json, reports overall score, names the bottleneck subsystem, and recommends 2-3 prioritized improvements that target the lowest-scoring subsystem. Frames bottleneck as 'candidate' not confirmed cause.
expectations: Runs `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/validate-harness.mjs --target /tmp/eval-existing-harness --json`
Reports an overall score (0-100) and names which of the 5 subsystems (instructions/state/verification/scope/lifecycle) is the bottleneck
Recommends 2-3 concrete prioritized improvements rather than a generic list
Does NOT claim the bottleneck is the proven cause of agent failures — uses language like 'candidate bottleneck' until confirmed by an agent session

evals.name: HTML Assessment Report
evals.prompt: Generate a shareable HTML harness assessment for /tmp/eval-target so I can send it to my team.
evals.expected_output: Invokes validate-harness.mjs with --target /tmp/eval-target --html <TARGET>/harness-assessment.html, writes a self-contained HTML file to the target directory, and reports the absolute path of the produced HTML to the user.
expectations: Runs validate-harness.mjs with both --target and --html flags
HTML output path is inside the target directory and reported as an absolute path
Output explicitly notes this is a structural benchmark, not a behavioral one, and that real effectiveness still requires before/after agent-session testing
Does NOT skip the framing about structural-vs-behavioral evaluation

evals.name: Force Overwrite Consent Flow
evals.prompt: Scaffold a harness in /tmp/eval-target — there's already an AGENTS.md there but I want to start fresh.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to silently pass --force. Uses AskUserQuestion to confirm whether to overwrite the existing AGENTS.md (and any other existing harness files), offering options for overwrite-this-file / skip / overwrite-all. Only after explicit user approval invokes create-harness.mjs with --force.
expectations: Detects the existing AGENTS.md before invoking create-harness.mjs with --force
Uses AskUserQuestion (not plain text) to surface the overwrite decision
Offers at minimum three options: overwrite this file, skip this file, overwrite all existing harness files
Does NOT invoke create-harness.mjs with --force before user confirms

evals.name: Go-only Repo Without package.json
evals.prompt: Scaffold a harness for my Go project at /tmp/eval-go-repo. There's no package.json — it's pure Go with a go.mod file.
evals.expected_output: Invokes create-harness.mjs with --target /tmp/eval-go-repo. Detector falls back to Go stack via go.mod presence. init.sh contains Go-flavored verification commands (e.g., `go build ./...`, `go test ./...`, `go vet ./...`) rather than npm/bun-flavored commands. AGENTS.md and other artifacts are still produced normally.
expectations: Runs create-harness.mjs successfully without failing on missing package.json
init.sh uses Go commands like `go test ./...` or `go build ./...` and does NOT contain `npm`, `bun`, `pnpm`, or `yarn` commands
AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, session-handoff.md, and init.sh are all written to /tmp/eval-go-repo

evals.name: Misuse — User Wants a Skill, Not a Harness
evals.prompt: Create a Claude Code skill for extracting tables from PDFs.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is a Claude Code skill request, not a harness scaffolding request. Does NOT invoke create-harness.mjs. Redirects the user to the forge-skill workflow (or stays inactive so forge-skill can pick it up).
expectations: Does NOT run `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/create-harness.mjs`
Does NOT produce AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, session-handoff.md, or init.sh
Names forge-skill (or create-skill) as the correct workflow for building a Claude Code skill
Distinguishes between scaffolding an agent harness in a target repo vs creating a reusable Claude Code skill primitive

evals.name: Laravel + Filament App With Both composer.json and package.json
evals.prompt: Scaffold a harness for my Laravel + Filament app at /tmp/eval-laravel. It has a composer.json plus a package.json for Vite assets.
evals.expected_output: Invokes create-harness.mjs with --target /tmp/eval-laravel. Detector resolves to the PHP/Laravel stack via composer.json + laravel/framework even though a package.json is present (the PHP backend is primary; package.json is just the Vite asset build). init.sh contains PHP verification commands (composer install, php artisan test, and ./vendor/bin/pint --test when Laravel Pint is present, or the project's composer scripts) rather than npm/bun-flavored commands. Reports the detected php-laravel stack.
expectations: Runs `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/create-harness.mjs --target /tmp/eval-laravel`
Reports the detected stack as PHP/Laravel (php-laravel), NOT node or typescript, despite the package.json being present

init.sh contains `composer install` and a PHP test command (`php artisan test`, `composer test`, or `./vendor/bin/pest`/`phpunit`) and does NOT contain `npm`, `bun`, `pnpm`, `yarn`, or `pytest` commands
AGENTS.md (or CLAUDE.md), feature_list.json, progress.md, session-handoff.md, and init.sh are all written

evals.name: Python + uv + Django Project
evals.prompt: Set up an agent harness for my Django project at /tmp/eval-django. It uses uv (there's a uv.lock and [tool.uv] in pyproject.toml) and has a manage.py.
evals.expected_output: Invokes create-harness.mjs with --target /tmp/eval-django. Detector resolves to the python-django stack via manage.py + django dependency. init.sh uses uv-managed runners (`uv sync`, then `uv run ...` prefixed commands) and Django's `python manage.py test` rather than a bare `python -m pytest`. ruff and ty checks are included when configured. Reports the detected stack.
expectations: Runs `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/create-harness.mjs --target /tmp/eval-django`
init.sh uses uv (`uv sync` and `uv run` prefixes) and Django's `python manage.py test`, and does NOT use a bare `python -m pytest`/`python -m compileall` or npm commands
Reports the detected stack as python-django
AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, progress.md, session-handoff.md, and init.sh are all written to /tmp/eval-django
